content.full_text:
F.q,rn Porrucar Pna.crrcps CouvussroN
P.O. Box 8O7 . 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-s660 . Fax (9t6) 322-0886
August 7,2000
Suzanne Du Vall Knorr, REHS
Box 8056
Glendale, CA 91224-0056
RT
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-00-156
Dear Ms. Knorr:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflicts
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because the Commission is only
empowered to offer technical assistance in regards to your question, we are treating your
request as one for informal assistance.2
QUESTION
What actions might you take to challenge the inclusion of Environmental Health
Specialist II in the newly amended conflict of interest code for Los Angeles County?
CONCLUSION
Under Section 87307, you may appeal the County's conflict of interest code,
established under Section 87300, directly to your agency. If your appeal is denied, you
may petition the County Board of Supervisors, the code reviewing body, within 30 days
after the denial. The Fair Political Practices Commission does not have authority to
amend the County's conflict of interest code.
' Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear attitle 2,sections
18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not p.oud" the requestor with the immunity provided in an opinion or
formal wriffen advice. (Section 831 14; Regulation 18329(c).)


Suzanne Du Vall Knorr
I-00-156
Page two
FACTS
You are an Environmental Health Specialist II for Los Angeles County. Recently,
the County amended its conflict of interest code to include your position iN a designated
employee who is required to file a Statement of Economic Interests (Form 700). you
believe that certain information required to be reported could place you and your husband
in physical danger.
ANALYSIS
The designation of employees under an agency's conflict of interest code is left to
the discretion of each individual agency and its resptictive code reviewing body.
(Sections 87300-87310.) Section 82011 indicates that the board of supervisors is the
code reviewing body with respect to the conflict of interest code of any county agency,
other than the board of supervisors, with jurisdiction wholly within the county.
Therefore, as noted above, the Commission cannot remove certain employeeiositions
from designation under a county's conflict of interest code. The Commission-is
empowered to provide technical assistance pursuant to section 87312. Although we may
provide assistance under Section 87312 upon request, please note that any suggestions
are not binding on the County or on the Board of Supervisors as the County's code
reviewing body.
Government Code section 87300 requires every agency to adopt and promulgate a
conflict of interest code. Conflict of interest codes ar"."quirra to iaerrtitr, designated
employees, and assign to those designated positions appropriate categories of financial
disclosure. Each conflict of interest code must specifically enumerate the positions within
the agency that are involved in making or participating in making governmental decisions
which may foreseeably have a material financial effect on any economic interest, and
further sets forth the types of investments, business positions, income, and real property
that are reportable by those designated employees. (Section 87302.)
The conflict of interest provisions of the Act apply only to "public officials,"
including "designated employees." (Section 87100.) A "public officiall' is defined by the
Act to include every member, officer, employee, or consultant of a state or local
government agency. (Section 82048.) The term "designated employee" means any
officer, employee, member, or consultant of any agency who makes or participates in the
making of agency decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on any
financial interest. (Section 32019.)
A public official makes a govemmental decision whenever the public official
votes on a matter, appoints a person, obligates or commits the agency to a course of
action, or enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of the agency. (Regulation
18700(b).) Additionally, a public official participates in a governmental decision when,


Suzanne Du Vall Knorr
I-00_156
Page three
acting within the authority of the official's position, the public official negotiates, advises,
or makes recortmendations to the decision-maker without significant substantive review
with respect to the actions enumerated in Regulation 18700(b). This includes conducting
research, making any investigation, or preparing or presenting any report, analysis, or
opinion which requires the exercise ofjudgment on the part of the official or designated
employee and the purpose of which is to influence the decision. (Regulation f aZOOlcy.;
Under Section 87307, any employee may petition his or her agency to amend its
conflict of interest code. If the appeal is denied, the employee may "pp.uito 
the code
reviewing body within 30 days of the denial. In your case, the rod" r.ui.*ing body is
the County Board of Supervisors.
Also, enclosed for your information is Regulation 1g740.
If you have any additional questions, please feel free to call me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant
By:
PhD
Political Reform Consultant II
Enclosure

Suzanne Du Vall Knorr
